fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Texas Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity texas, how to transfer physical therapist license to texas, physical therapist compact texas, texas physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Texas Executive Council of PT and OT Examiners (ECPTOTE)
board.parent_dept: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Texas.

steps.title: Register with the Online Licensing System
steps.description: Create an account on the Texas Board of Physical Therapy Examiners' Online Licensing System portal (https://www.licensing.hpc.texas.gov/hpcclient/portal/hpcclient/hpc_web.jsp?agencyId=900) by selecting 'First Time User? Register'.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your account on the Online Licensing System and complete the Physical Therapist licensure application, submitting all required information and paying the application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, confirming your physical therapy education and degree, to be sent directly from your accredited educational institution to the Texas Board of Physical Therapy Examiners.

steps.title: Pass the Texas Jurisprudence Exam (JP Exam)
steps.description: Successfully complete the Texas Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Exam, which is an online exam assessing knowledge of state laws and rules related to physical therapy practice.

steps.title: Pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)
steps.description: Register for and pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) administered by the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT). Ensure your scores are sent to the Texas Board of Physical Therapy Examiners.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprinting and Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Complete the required fingerprinting process through Identogo for a criminal background check as mandated by the Texas Board of Physical Therapy Examiners.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Texas physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $190. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Texas have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Texas participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Texas without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Texas?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 3 to 4 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Texas
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Texas, applicants must register and complete their application through the Texas Board of Physical Therapy Examiners' Online Licensing System.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Go to the Online Licensing System portal. 2. Click on 'First Time User? Register'. 3. Follow the prompts to create a User ID and password. 4. Log in with your new credentials to start an application.
